analysis.problem: Inefficient grid modernization efforts and lack of investment in renewable energy may hinder the transition to a sustainable energy system
analysis.root_cause: The root cause of this problem is the lack of coordination and investment in grid communication infrastructure, as well as the prioritization of fossil fuel projects over renewable energy
leverage_points: Utility companies and grid operators have the greatest effect on the problem

proposed_actions.action: Increase investment in grid communication infrastructure and renewable energy projects
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Achieve a 20% increase in renewable energy capacity and a 15% reduction in grid interference within the next 2 years

evidence: The Utility Broadband Alliance is expanding its membership to enhance grid communication infrastructure
The Trump administration is buying back offshore wind leases and redirecting funds to fossil fuel projects
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domains of Civilization and Cosmos, as a sustainable energy system is crucial for the long-term viability of intelligent life and its environments
analysis.summary: Inefficient grid modernization efforts and lack of investment in renewable energy may hinder the transition to a sustainable energy system, and increasing investment in grid communication infrastructure and renewable energy projects can help address this problem

raw_items.title: New UBBA members showcase why communication networks are foundational to grid modernization efforts
raw_items.summary: The Utility Broadband Alliance expanded its membership with organizations like Eversource Energy and Hawaiian Electric to enhance grid communication infrastructure.

raw_items.title: What does global energy trade look like after US-Iran deal? – This Week in Cleantech
raw_items.summary: On this episode of the This Week in Cleantech podcast, Tim McDonnell from Semafor discusses the potential future of the Strait of Hormuz and energy markets if the US-Iran de-escalation deal holds.

raw_items.title: Trump administration to buy back another energy company’s offshore wind leases for 4 more projects
raw_items.summary: The Trump administration is buying back Invenergy's four offshore wind leases for $765 million, redirecting funds to fossil fuel projects.

raw_items.title: FERC takes historic action, orders US grid operators to ‘defend or revise’ large load interconnection tariffs
raw_items.summary: FERC has issued tailored show-cause orders under section 206 of the Federal Power Act to each of the six regional grid operators within its jurisdiction, directing them to either justify or reform the rules governing how large energy users like data centers connect to the electric grid.

raw_items.title: Solar Is the Cheapest Power in History, But States Are Retreating From It
raw_items.summary: A high-stakes push-and-pull is taking place in the United States clean energy sector as state-level laws, federal-level policy, and that infamous invisible hand all tug in different directions. A staggering amount of different legal actions on the part of the Trump administration, the judicial system, and state politicians have created an incredibly complex landscape for clean energy adoption across the country. raw_items.title: Why Lunar Helium-3 Mining Still Can't Compete With Earth
raw_items.summary: When it comes to Helium-3, the biggest cost divide is between Earth and the Moon. Potential sources range from tritium decay and terrestrial helium wells on Earth to lunar regolith on the Moon. Today, Earth-based sources remain far easier and cheaper to access. This graphic, created by Visual Capitalist's Cody Good in partnership with Pulsar Helium, compares major potential sources of Helium-3 by cost, scalability, and accessibility.
